description: "Fritz" is a courageous, up-to-the-minute youngster, with a big mouth, a little body, and a keen eye for beauty. In every sense of the word he is an all-round man—mostly "round." Fritz has the misfortune of being the baby of the law class and on more than one occasion this year, Mr. Morris has served as a most efficient nurse. Fritz's social activities are only limited by the fact that some girls will persist in growing up. If we only had "Hans," the comic side of the law department would be complete. Fritz upon first entering the university had the wrong impression of how to get a college education, especially under Sylvanus. Becoming weary of having his "goat" tied up in the dean's office, he finally obtained its honorable discharge, together with a set of law books awarded to the student showing the most improvement for the year.

name: Powell, Julian William
description: Maybe there has been a time in Billie's life when he was worried about something, but honestly we don't believe it. An earthquake might make him a trifle peevish, but as for worrying, positively no! J. W. knows how to take care of himself, but if one should judge by outward appearances it would seem as if he needs a guardian. Since his arrival five years ago he has collected two degrees, and four football 'G's' with about as little effort as it takes an ordinary man to pass a course under Odum. He always makes it a rule to study once a month no matter whether there is an exam. or not, that is, of course, if there is nothing else to do, but don't think for a minute he ever gets shot. For he is always late. Billie has succeeded in doing many things, but in one he has failed. He just can't convince H. Abit that he is chronically ill and puny by nature.

name: Quarterman, Jr., William Henry
description: William is some boy as an academic student, but as a lawyer he would make a great soda-jerker. Personally we like Bill very much, but we are afraid our opinion is not concurred in entirely by our beloved faculty, especially Hosea Abit. Anyway, Willie Henry is the speed king of the class without a doubt. He can arise from his morning slumbers in the Road Laboratory when the doctor starts calling the roll and is able to dress, shave, eat breakfast, and reach class by the time the old gentleman gets to the 'Q's.' He will have seen all of his class-mates at least three times during the year: the first day of school, the law banquet, and commencement day.

name: James Wilmer Ray, LL.B.
description: James hails from the lovely suburban metropolis of Monroe and has been with us off and on for the last six years. Most people think he's hopeless, but really he's not. The dizzy whirl of Athens society has simply proved too much for him. He'll be all right when he gets back on the farm and forgets all about pinchback coats and No. 11 dancing pumps. He'll gradually outgrow then his present burning ambition to beat Polly Peachtree out of her job. Everybody thinks he is "tight," but really he is not. He swore off early in life from buying gasoline, and is familiarly known as "Gasoline Bug Ray."

name: Alvan Baker Rowe, LL.B.
description: Several years ago there was a little boy whose parents lived in the city of Savannah. One day his mother sent him over to a neighbor's to get an axe which had been borrowed and never returned. A lad equally as young answered the door bell. The visitor gazed into the questioning face of his host for a minute or more, and then summoned courage to say: "My ma axed me to ax you to ax your ma for the axe, is you gwine to ax her fer me?" Alvan Baker is one of our younger boys, and while he has not yet gotten over his childish ways, we believe some day he will yet be a man.

name: George Adams Shuford
description: George is not nearly so innocent as he looks. He claims to play no favorites among the ladies, but just the same we notice he's always to be found on lovers row beside Dulaney, Dunn, Theus and Cooper at Sylvie's morning matinees. G. A. is a good sport himself, but he says the other boys aren't because they won't patronize the dames in the daytime. He patronizes them at all times himself, having tacked a permanent position on the door and realizing that it is his duty to be good to the girls. By the way, we'll tell you a secret. George has the knack for making money that you never would suspect. We expect to see him build a better hotel than the Grove Park Inn some day.

name: Vaughan Adolphus Sims
description: Our book agent is about ready to open up an office on the graft he secured from law students. However, we blame him not. He has been greatly handicapped by having to room with Trapnell, but we firmly believe that a partnership composed of these two would, so far as consistency is concerned, be a gem. Sims' favorite exercise is carving steak at Denmark Hall, and has developed a wonderful physique as a result. Sims is a man who attends to his own business, and this will be the secret of his success in the practice of law. We are expecting great things of Vaughnam Adolphus.

name: Emmet Arnold Skelton
description: The secret of Skelt's success in his work in the law department is his independence and absolute indifference to the profs. He never asks any questions, no matter how important, and always having a grave and serious expression overshadowing his Charlie Chaplin countenance. Skelton would be a great patriot only for one reason. He says he cannot get a military uniform to fit him as snugly as one of Hart, Schafner and Mark's latest. Emmet is one of the few who stick to the ancient style of parting his hair in the middle. There has been some discussion as to why he does not change. Some say he would outstrip a fair maid in the rural district of Hart county, while others contend that he thinks he resembles the dean of our faculty.

name: Seward Mallette Smith
description: Seward M. Smith hails from Homerville, Ga., and is otherwise known as Marion. He is a fair specimen from the Okeefenokee swamp (sneeze it). The Okeefenokee has for quite a while been Marion's garden of Eden, but since entering the university he has cast off his swampy costume and now boasts a suit for each day of the week. Marion is a hard worker and we predict for him a most successful future as a J. P. of 989 G. M. District. He has devoted his time entirely to his law work, and up to date remains a "non." Marion has lots of friends when a "fish" in billiards is needed. However, taking everything into consideration, Smith is among the best (in his own estimation).

name: Willum Harry Spillers
description: His real name is Willum, and he insists on being so called, but his friends call him "Billcos" for short. His howls from the Methodist church choir, his expert ability in binding books and fixing signs and notices in the library, his ceaseless reading of Common Law Pleading, his tireless efforts towards shooting Sylvanus—all guarantee him to be a lawyer of the first rank (very rank, in fact!) He is such a compendium of all the virtues and so self-centered that in the volume of himself there is no room for an appendix. From the rasping noise he makes in trying to make the Juniors be quiet one would very likely think that the doctor forgot something like a saw in the performance of the operation. We admire a man with a backbone like Harry has.

name: Charlie Mabry Tanner
description: The writer does not know much about Tanner and is therefore afraid to say much lest it might be true, but perhaps had he been to class more often we would have become better acquainted with this talented fellow-classman. He is quick on the trigger and scarcely ever fails to shoot—when he happens to be about and the opportunity presents itself. We might say in passing that he seems to have become a permanent fixture at the university several years ago. "Snake" was the first to hold the office of president in the "Manicure Club" for this year, and his term was most successful.

name: Charlton Mayer Theus, LL.B.
description: Poor Babe! If he would just use his head some time and not depend so much on his memory he would be the most brilliant student that ever graced the university. He studies more than Ormonde and he worries more than everybody else in the university put together. Sylvie has a strong suspicion that Mr. Theus never owned a law book but spends all his time rushing the girls and putting on clean collars and replenishing his pockets with clean handkerchiefs. Consequently he always drags out his forty-two centimeter and do what "Babe" will, shot he is. If earnestness, seriousness and unlimited energy and perseverance count for anything at all Charlton will be a great man some day.

name: James Comer Trapnell, LL.B.
description: Comer is one of that kind of fellows who, when you first meet him you don't know whether he is blessed with his overshare of ignorance, or is just the opposite—a "wise bird." He keeps his mouth shut, feeling that he needs no advice about his own business (including love affairs) and is slow to advise. Comer's birthplace, and they say it still exists, is Metter, Ga., which is fourteen miles removed from the closest R. F. D. Those who have gone into this interior district say that the town is habited with innumerable pretty maidens. If this be so, we see no reason why "Trap" should not get the best as he is a regular "city slicker" now, and when he returns ought to startle the "natives" and get off to a good start in the raising of Sea-Island cotton and the practice of law.

name: Burton Lee Weston
motto: "By his pipe ye shall know him,"
description: Burton's figure is quite familiar to the South Georgia swamps and every pesty rattlesnake and crocodile knows him by sight and run from his gun. Quiet and methodical, unless aroused, he usually is successful, whether that success is obtained in the class room, in advocating the honor system, boosting Teddy Roosevelt, or in winning the heart of his landlady's daughter, it matters little with him. "By his pipe ye shall know him," even long before he looms up in sight. Weston, since he has had the priviledge of "wearing a Senior cane," looks the part of a Kentucky colonel, and has the honor of being the most dignified member of the Senior law class, nothing about his demeanor being open to criticism.

name: William Pickney Welchel
description: "Pinkey" is a live-wire about some things; about others he is overwhelmingly indifferent. Pinkey's activities, so Mr. Morris thinks are confined solely to an "ambulatory minstrel," he and Ed Soule holding down the scenery. He and Abe Goldstein are great rivals in respect to their noses. After careful thought and deliberation it is conceded that the prize should go to Pinkey. Pinkey's home is Gainesville, which is incidentally the location of Brenau College, and owing to the atmosphere in which he was brought up has many socialistic tendencies, which he demonstrates on many occasions here.

name: Charles Alvin Whitten
description: "Whit" is neither crazily bashful nor sincerely brazen, but is a good compound of both; he is humorous on occasions (he admits it himself); the fact is he sometimes affords amusement in the recitation room, to the joy of his "Profs." and the relief of his class-mates. He has an ambition to make a million, but we are afraid he is too lazy to do so. "Whit" has a passion for fair women and loud shirts and frequently uses the latter to offset his lack of beauty and manly physique. "Whit" also indulges in "big words," which he always succeeds in using at the wrong time, although few find the error until they consult a dictionary. He is entirely original, never using one which has come to the observation of any law student.

name: Hugh Miller Willett, Jr.
description: He is known as the most desperate lover ever produced by the law department, and if he can write enough insurance to make it worth the lady's while, it is rumored that he will soon be as hen-pecked and bossed as the average married man. Hugh is an active worker among the Y. M. C. A., and to this we accredit his boot-lick with Dean Morris. Hugh is a very affectionate boy, always encircling you with his arms when near you, and we are curious to know if these inclinations cling to him when in company with the fairer sex.

name: Boyce Moultrie Gilbert
description: Another dope mixer! But he is a good one. Anyone that can roll bread pills for a living and come ahead is pretty good, with bread at its present high price. Now, we don't want to say anything bad about Boyce, but in all truthfulness we must say that he is the "low downest" base singer that ever filled a berth on the "Scrap-Iron Four." Anybody who hears him sing can easily tell that he eats at the "Beanery." Garfield should be proud to claim this handsome and accomplished castor oil dispenser. We hate to see him go back to the country.

name: Harold Clair Gilbert
description: "Hap 'air, fellers!" How often are we greeted thus, when walking to and from classes we run into this wonder of the pill-rolling profession. As light of spirit as he is of mind, Gilbert always has a smile for those whom he may meet. He must have brought his habit of speaking to everyone he meets from the metropolis called Washington, Georgia, a burg not quite so renowned as its name. Although he has been with us only two years, he has, in that time, learned more about mixing dope than any sane human would dare to risk himself with. Harold tells us that he is not so good at keeping books, but adds that he will probably have no use for books after he has been out of school for a little while. We feel that he is too modest, however, and much as we hate to see him go, we must compliment the State upon her addition of another worthy citizen.

name: Charles Bert Gordon Sweatland
description: Sounds like a Danish prayer, but it isn't. It is just another pharmaceutical menace to the university. Although not reared among us, he has grown to be one of us. Dave Paddock brought him down to us, and closely following his arrival, comes the rumor that he is desperately in love with an automobile. We feel sure that Charles Bert Gordon Sweatland will make Pennsylvania a distinguished and capable compounder and dispenser of medicines. Any undertaker could do far worse than have Sweatland as his ally.

name: Charles Toliver Witcher
description: Another plow-boy ruined! For over a year's time Charley walked about the campus while everybody thought he was being exposed to agriculture. His reason for taking pharmacy is that farming is too hot for a man of his disposition, so he has chiefly occupied his time in trying to perfect a successful hair restorer. After a very heated and forceful argument, Toliver has been convinced that the walks about the campus were made by the hand of man and not by mother nature. If you wish to be quietly and painlessly removed, buy your pills from Witcher.
